origin.name: Japan
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Unexpected, unnatural, or suspicious deaths. Also deaths outside hospital.
origin.death_certificate_name: Shibo todoke (death notification) and Shibo kenshin-sho (death certificate)
origin.death_certificate_language: Japanese only. Certified English translation required.
origin.embalming_note: Embalming is not standard practice in Japan. Must be specifically arranged. Japanese funeral homes use dry ice as standard. Request embalming explicitly.
origin.cremation_note: Cremation rate in Japan is over 99%. Returning ashes home is a well-established and straightforward option.
origin.key_document: Shibo kenshin-sho (Japanese death certificate) with certified English translation
origin.source: FCDO Japan travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Tokyo guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Shibo kenshin-sho (Japanese death certificate) with certified English translation
special_rules: Death certificate is in Japanese only. Certified English translation is mandatory.
Embalming must be specifically requested. Japanese funeral homes use dry ice as standard. Embalming is required for international repatriation.
Bringing ashes home (cremation in Japan) is significantly faster and simpler than full body repatriation.
Medical examiner (kansetsu-i) involvement for unexpected deaths outside hospital.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Japanese documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Tokyo can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Japan to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Japan. Repatriation to the UK takes 10-21 days. Japanese documentation and direct flights from Tokyo explained. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: Shibo kenshin-sho with certified English translation

airlines.name: Japan Airlines
airlines.routes: NRT to DUB (via LHR or FRA)

special_rules: Irish Embassy in Tokyo handles consular matters.
Embalming must be specifically arranged as in UK repatriations.
Certified English translation of shibo kenshin-sho required.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Japanese documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Tokyo can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Japan to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Japan and needs to come home to Ireland. 10-21 days. Japanese documentation and Tokyo cargo flights handled. Contact us 24/7.
